title: Interest Rates and Fees: A property that generates more income than its expenses is...
summary: Distinguish positive from negative leverage based on property income vs. debt costs.
prompt: A property that generates more income than its expenses is said to be in a state of negative leverage.
solution: False. Negative leverage occurs when the cost of debt exceeds the return generated by the property. In contrast, if a property generates more income than its expenses, it is considered to have positive leverage. Understanding leverage dynamics is important for finance students as it impacts investment strategies and risk management. Positive leverage enhances returns on equity, while negative leverage can diminish returns.
